inspectables.label: Left eye marker
inspectables.description: A schematic eye marker anchors the viewer's hand-authored left-eye nasal-retinal route. Its position and size are illustrative, not measured orbital or retinal anatomy.

relationships.summary: The drawn nasal-retinal route runs from this eye marker to the optic chiasm before crossing toward the contralateral LGN.

inspectables.label: Right eye marker
inspectables.description: A schematic eye marker anchors the viewer's hand-authored right-eye nasal-retinal route. Its position and size are illustrative, not measured orbital or retinal anatomy.

relationships.summary: The drawn nasal-retinal route runs from this eye marker to the optic chiasm before crossing toward the contralateral LGN.

inspectables.label: Optic chiasm
inspectables.description: The optic chiasm is the midline junction where nasal-retinal axons cross while temporal-retinal axons remain uncrossed. The viewer marks it schematically and currently draws only the crossing nasal routes.

relationships.summary: Nasal-retinal fibres cross at the chiasm and continue in the contralateral optic tract toward the LGN.

inspectables.label: Optic radiation
inspectables.description: The optic radiation is the posterior thalamocortical projection carrying LGN relay-cell axons through the retrolenticular internal capsule toward primary visual cortex.

relationships.summary: The displayed projection runs from the LGN toward V1.

sources.label: Maciag et al. 2024 — visual-pathway anatomy and localization
Maciag et al. 2024 — visual-pathway anatomy and localization

inspectables.label: Lateral geniculate nucleus (LGN)
inspectables.description: The lateral geniculate nucleus is a paired thalamic visual nucleus that preserves an ordered representation of the contralateral visual field while regulating retinocortical transmission.

relationships.summary: LGN relay-cell axons project toward V1 through the optic radiation.

sources.label: Maciag et al. 2024 — visual-pathway anatomy and localization

inspectables.label: Primary visual cortex (V1)
inspectables.description: Primary visual cortex lies mainly along calcarine cortex, contains a retinotopic representation of the contralateral visual field, and transforms local visual structure.

sources.label: Hubel & Wiesel 1962 — V1 receptive fields and functional architecture
Benson et al. 2012 — human V1 retinotopy and variation

inspectables.label: Superficial white matter
inspectables.description: The displayed superficial-white-matter layer is a broad sample of short bilateral tractography contours. The viewer can subset contours by unordered geometric endpoint assignments to selected Jülich maximum-probability labels, but those assignments do not establish named U-fibres, biological terminations, or region-to-region connections.

sources.label: Yeh et al. 2021 — streamline termination and node-assignment limits

inspectables.label: ILF
inspectables.description: The displayed ILF is a bilateral 180-streamline-per-hemisphere sample from the HCP-1065 population-averaged association bundle. Its supported relationships report only low-confidence endpoint proximity to selected displayed Jülich shells; streamline order supplies no polarity.

relationships.summary: In the displayed sample, 89 left and 95 right ILF streamlines have an unordered endpoint nearest to the Temporo-parietal (unparc.) shell within 3 mm; the 5 mm counts are 99 left and 137 right. This is a qualified proximity observation, not a measured termination or connection strength.

sources.label: Yeh et al. 2021 — streamline termination and node-assignment limits

relationships.summary: In the displayed sample, 71 left and 27 right ILF streamlines have an unordered endpoint nearest to the STS2 shell within 3 mm; the 5 mm counts are 74 left and 28 right. This is a qualified proximity observation, not a measured termination or connection strength.

sources.label: Yeh et al. 2021 — streamline termination and node-assignment limits

inspectables.label: Broca 44
inspectables.description: This displayed atlas shell represents 44 in the inferior frontal gyrus group. Relationship records report only low-confidence proximity of sampled displayed bundle endpoints to this shell, not measured connectivity, function, or an individual cortical boundary.
